input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-05-08T01:00:12.477Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

This reacts automatically when the device theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties (shorthand)
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Light: Colors.Black,
    Dark:  Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

// For any property type
myButton.SetAppTheme<Color>(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Light: Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Dark:  Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` automatically |
| **Resource dictionaries** | You can also theme via `AppThemeBinding` inside merged `ResourceDictionary` styles for reuse |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to override system preference |
| **Detect changes in code** | Subscribe to `Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged` |

For larger apps, define themed colors in `App.xaml` resources and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` instead of repeating hex values on every element.
